var.name: JWT_ADMIN_ACCESS_EXPIRES_IN
var.note: Admin tokens expire sooner per frontend spec

var.name: ACTIVATION_TOKEN_TTL_H
var.note: Hours — matches frontend spec: 24h activation window

var.name: SITE_URL
var.note: SvelteKit dev server. In production: https://dlab.usfq.edu.ec

var.name: SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY
var.note: Use service_role key — server-side only. NEVER expose to SvelteKit client-side.

var.name: STORAGE_PROVIDER
var.note: Controls which IStorageProvider implementation is injected at startup.

file.name: db.js
file.note: mysql2 createPool factory — reads DB_* env vars. Exports pool.

file.name: env.js
file.note: dotenv.config() + zod schema validation at startup. Throws on missing required vars.

dir.note: IStorageProvider interface pattern — swap Supabase for Azure without touching business logic.

file.name: SupabaseStorageProvider.js
file.note: Implements IStorageProvider using @supabase/supabase-js storage API. MVP active implementation.

file.name: AzureStorageProvider.js
file.note: STUB — all methods throw AppError(ERR_STORAGE_NOT_IMPLEMENTED, 501). Swap in when Azure credentials ready. Future deps: @azure/storage-blob.

file.name: storageFactory.js
file.note: Reads STORAGE_PROVIDER env var. Returns SupabaseStorageProvider or AzureStorageProvider singleton. Throws on unknown value.

file.name: upload.js
file.note: multer({ storage: multer.memoryStorage() }) configured with size + MIME validation. NO disk writes.

file.name: errorHandler.js
file.note: Global Express error handler. Maps AppError to HTTP response. Hides stack in production.

file.name: rateLimiter.js
file.note: express-rate-limit. Applied to auth mutation routes.

file.name: auditLogger.js
file.note: Middleware/helper that writes to audit_log table after successful mutations.

file.name: email.service.js
file.note: Resend wrappers: sendActivation, sendAdminNewRegistration, sendCollaboratorApproved, sendCollaboratorRejected, sendPasswordReset

file.name: media.service.js
file.note: Calls storageFactory singleton. Resolves bucket by resource_type. Manages media_assets DB record.

file.name: AppError.js
file.note: class AppError extends Error { constructor(code, message, httpStatus) }

file.name: jwt.utils.js
file.note: signAccessToken, signRefreshToken, verifyToken helpers. Separate functions for admin vs collaborator role claim.

file.name: slug.utils.js
file.note: generateSlug(title) — URL-safe, unique via DB check

file.name: app.js
file.note: Express app bootstrap: cors, helmet, morgan, json body parser, routes, errorHandler.

interface.name: IStorageProvider
interface.purpose: Defines the contract all storage backends must implement. Business logic always calls this interface — never a concrete provider directly.

param.name: bucket
param.note: Resolved bucket name from env. e.g. pmi-frontend-proj-images

param.name: buffer
param.note: File buffer from multer memoryStorage — no temp files on disk

implementation.name: SupabaseStorageProvider
implementation.error-handling: Supabase error response → throw new AppError(ERR_STORAGE_UPLOAD_FAILED, 502)

implementation.name: AzureStorageProvider
implementation.all-methods-throw: new AppError(ERR_STORAGE_NOT_IMPLEMENTED, "Azure storage not yet configured.", 501)
implementation.activation-note: Remove stub throws, implement BlobServiceClient calls, set STORAGE_PROVIDER=azure in env.

storage-interface.object-path-convention: Format: {resource_type}/{uuidv4()}-{sanitize(original_filename)}.{ext} Examples: projects/a1b2c3d4-hero-image.webp events/e5f6g7h8-workshop-banner.png Sanitize: lowercase, replace spaces with hyphens, strip special chars, max 80 chars.

response-envelope.note: All responses — including 4xx/5xx — use this envelope.

entry.meaning: Bad Request — validation failure or missing params
Unauthenticated — missing, invalid, or expired token
Forbidden — valid token but insufficient role
Conflict — duplicate email, slug, or constraint violation
Too Many Requests — rate limit hit
Not Implemented — storage provider stub called
Bad Gateway — upstream Supabase or Resend failure

auth-conventions.access-token: Short-lived JWT (15m). Sent as Authorization: Bearer <token> header.
auth-conventions.refresh-token: Long-lived JWT (7d collaborator / 1d admin). Stored in httpOnly, SameSite=Strict cookie named refresh_token. Hashed copy stored in refresh_tokens table.
auth-conventions.role-claim: JWT payload: { sub: userId, role: "collaborator"|"admin", iat, exp }

cors.methods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS

global-conventions.security-headers: helmet() defaults — CSP, X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, HSTS in production
global-conventions.audit-log-rule: All admin mutations (create, update, delete, status_change, approve, reject, assign, unassign) must call audit.queries.insertAuditLog({ actor_type, actor_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, previous_value, new_value, description, ip_address }) after successful DB write. Written by service layer, not triggers.

item.name: verifyAccessToken
item.steps: 1. Extract Authorization header; split "Bearer <token>". 2. If missing → 401 ERR_TOKEN_MISSING. 3. jwt.verify(token, JWT_ACCESS_SECRET). 4. If TokenExpiredError → 401 ERR_TOKEN_EXPIRED. 5. If JsonWebTokenError → 401 ERR_TOKEN_INVALID. 6. If payload.role !== "collaborator" → 403 ERR_FORBIDDEN. 7. Attach req.user = { id: payload.sub, role: "collaborator" }. Call next().

item.name: verifyAdminToken
item.steps: 1–5. Same token extraction and verification steps as above. 6. Verify payload.role === "admin". If not → 403 ERR_FORBIDDEN. 7. Attach req.admin = { id: payload.sub, role: "admin" }. Call next().

item.name: upload
item.no-disk-writes: Files live only in req.file.buffer (RAM) until passed to IStorageProvider.upload(). Express process never writes to disk.

item.name: rateLimiter
item.config: windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, max: 20, standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false

item.name: errorHandler

email-service.error-handling: Resend API error → throw new AppError(ERR_EMAIL_SEND_FAILED, 502). Non-blocking for registration (log + continue).
email-service.templates: HTML is inline template literal functions. React Email upgrade is a future scope item.

template.name: sendActivationEmail
template.subject: Activa tu cuenta en DLAB USFQ
template.body-summary: Welcome message, activation CTA button, 24h expiry warning, contact link.

template.name: sendAdminNewRegistration
template.to: all active administrators — SELECT personal_email FROM administrators WHERE is_active = true
template.subject: Nueva solicitud de colaborador — [firstName lastName]
template.vars: firstName, lastName, email, major, current_university_year, adminReviewUrl (SITE_URL + /admin/colaboradores/ID)
template.body-summary: Registration summary card with direct link to admin review page.

template.name: sendCollaboratorApproved
template.subject: ¡Fuiste aprobado en DLAB! Activa tu cuenta
template.body-summary: Approval confirmation, activation link, next steps summary.

template.name: sendCollaboratorRejected
template.subject: Actualización sobre tu solicitud a DLAB
template.body-summary: Respectful rejection message, encouragement to reapply, contact link.

template.name: sendPasswordReset
template.subject: Restablecer contraseña — DLAB USFQ
template.body-summary: Reset link with expiry warning. Security note: if not requested, ignore.

endpoint.description: Register a new collaborator via the /signup interest form. Account is created with is_active=false, password_hash=NULL, trajectory_status=nuevo. Password is NOT collected here — it is set later during account activation.

field.name: tag_ids
field.note: IDs from tags table — collaborator interest tags

responses.response: { "data": { "message": "Revisa tu correo para activar tu cuenta." } }
Field validation failure — returns field-level errors

side-effects.effect: INSERT INTO collaborators — trajectory_status=nuevo, is_active=false, password_hash=NULL
INSERT INTO collaborator_tags for each tag_id provided
Generate HMAC activation token via token.utils → INSERT INTO activation_tokens (collaborator_id, token_hash, expires_at)
email.sendActivationEmail(collaborator) — async, non-blocking
email.sendAdminNewRegistration(collaborator) — async, non-blocking, to all active admins

endpoint.description: Activate account using the HMAC token from the email link. Sets the collaborator password for the first time. Corresponds to /activate route in SvelteKit frontend (form action: setPassword).

field.name: token
field.note: Raw HMAC token from email URL query param

responses.response: Token does not match stored hash
Token exists but expires_at is in the past

side-effects.effect: Verify HMAC token: hash input token, compare against activation_tokens.token_hash where used=false
Check expires_at > NOW()
bcrypt.hash(password, BCRYPT_ROUNDS) → UPDATE collaborators SET password_hash, is_active=true, updated_at=NOW()
UPDATE activation_tokens SET used=true, used_at=NOW() WHERE token_hash=hash

endpoint.description: Authenticate collaborator. Returns access token + sets httpOnly refresh cookie.

responses.response: Wrong email or password — never specify which

side-effects.effect: SELECT collaborator WHERE personal_email = email (or usfq_email)
bcrypt.compare(password, password_hash)
signAccessToken({ sub: id, role: "collaborator" }, JWT_ACCESS_SECRET, JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRES_IN)
signRefreshToken → hash → INSERT INTO refresh_tokens (user_type=collaborator, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, user_agent, ip_address)
Set httpOnly refresh_token cookie

endpoint.description: Invalidate session. Clears refresh token from DB and cookie.

side-effects.effect: Read refresh_token cookie → hash → DELETE FROM refresh_tokens WHERE token_hash=hash AND user_id=req.user.id
Clear refresh_token cookie (set maxAge=0)

endpoint.description: Issue new access token via refresh token rotation. Detects token reuse (stolen token protection).

responses.response: Token not found in DB or hash mismatch
Token was already used → revoke ALL sessions for this user (security incident)

side-effects.effect: Read refresh_token cookie → hash → SELECT FROM refresh_tokens
If is_revoked OR token hash not found → revoke all tokens for user → 401 ERR_TOKEN_REUSE
If valid: DELETE old token row, INSERT new token row (rotation), set new cookie, return new access_token

endpoint.description: Request password reset email. Always returns 200 regardless of whether email exists to prevent email enumeration attacks (per frontend spec).

responses.response: { "data": { "message": "Si tu correo está registrado y activo, recibirás un enlace en los próximos minutos." } }

side-effects.effect: SELECT collaborator WHERE personal_email=email AND is_active=true
If found: generate HMAC reset token → INSERT INTO password_reset_tokens (collaborator_id, token_hash, expires_at) — TTL: RESET_TOKEN_TTL_M minutes
email.sendPasswordReset(collaborator) — only if found

endpoint.description: Complete password reset flow. Token is HMAC from reset email link (reuses /activate route pattern with mode=reset query param in frontend).

responses.response: Passwords do not match or policy violation

side-effects.effect: Verify HMAC token against password_reset_tokens
bcrypt.hash(new_password, BCRYPT_ROUNDS) → UPDATE collaborators SET password_hash, updated_at
Mark reset token used
UPDATE refresh_tokens SET is_revoked=true WHERE user_type=collaborator AND user_id=id (force re-login on all devices)

endpoint.description: Authenticate administrator. Returns access token (8h) + httpOnly refresh cookie (1d).

responses.response: Email exists in collaborators but not administrators

side-effects.effect: SELECT FROM administrators WHERE personal_email=email OR usfq_email=email AND is_active=true
bcrypt.compare
signAccessToken({ sub: id, role: "admin" }, JWT_ACCESS_SECRET, JWT_ADMIN_ACCESS_EXPIRES_IN)
INSERT INTO refresh_tokens (user_type=administrator, ...)
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=login
DELETE refresh token row + clear cookie
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=login (logout event)

endpoint.description: Refresh admin access token. Same rotation + reuse detection as collaborator refresh.
Get own full profile including tags, trajectory_status, assignment count.
endpoint.query: SELECT c.*, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT t.id) as tag_ids, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT t.name) as tag_names FROM collaborators c LEFT JOIN collaborator_tags ct ON ct.collaborator_id = c.id LEFT JOIN tags t ON t.id = ct.tag_id WHERE c.id = req.user.id GROUP BY c.id

responses.response: Should not occur if token is valid; safety guard.

endpoint.description: Update own editable profile fields. Read-only fields (email, trajectory_status) are ignored even if sent.

side-effects.effect: UPDATE collaborators SET editable fields WHERE id=req.user.id
DELETE FROM collaborator_tags WHERE collaborator_id=req.user.id; re-INSERT new tag_ids

endpoint.description: List own applications with project snapshot. Matches /my-applications frontend route.
endpoint.query: SELECT ap.*, p.title as project_title, p.slug as project_slug, p.status as project_status FROM applications ap JOIN projects p ON p.id = ap.project_id WHERE ap.collaborator_id = req.user.id [AND ap.status = :status if provided] ORDER BY ap.applied_at DESC

endpoint.description: Apply to a project. Requires trajectory_status to be activo (or contactado) per entity model. Corresponds to /projects/[slug]/apply frontend route form action.

responses.response: trajectory_status is not activo or contactado
project_id does not exist or is_visible=false

side-effects.effect: Verify collaborator.trajectory_status IN (activo, contactado, vinculado)
Verify project.status = activo AND project.is_visible = true
INSERT INTO applications (collaborator_id, project_id, reason_for_applying, status=pendiente, applied_at=NOW())

endpoint.description: Withdraw own application. Only allowed when status=pendiente.

responses.response: status is not pendiente (already under review)
Application not found or belongs to another collaborator

side-effects.effect: UPDATE applications SET status=retirada, updated_at=NOW() WHERE id=applicationId AND collaborator_id=req.user.id AND status=pendiente

endpoint.description: Get own availability slots.
Replace all availability slots (full replacement pattern).

field.name: time_to
field.note: HH:MM format, must be after time_from

side-effects.effect: DELETE FROM availability_slots WHERE collaborator_id=req.user.id; INSERT new slots in transaction

endpoint.description: List publicly visible projects. Powers /projects page with filter bar.
endpoint.query: SELECT p.id, p.title, p.slug, p.short_description, p.header_image_url, p.status, p.is_highlighted, p.participation_mode, p.current_collaborator_count, p.max_collaborators, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT t.name) as tag_names, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT t.slug) as tag_slugs FROM projects p LEFT JOIN project_tags pt ON pt.project_id = p.id LEFT JOIN tags t ON t.id = pt.tag_id WHERE p.is_visible = true [AND p.status = :status] [AND t.slug = :tag] [AND (p.title LIKE :search OR p.short_description LIKE :search)] GROUP BY p.id ORDER BY p.is_highlighted DESC, p.created_at DESC LIMIT :limit OFFSET :offset

endpoint.description: Full project detail. Powers /projects/[slug] page. Public — excludes private collaborator data.
endpoint.query: SELECT p.*, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT t.name ORDER BY t.name) as tag_names, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT pmd.day_of_week) as meeting_days, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM assignments a WHERE a.project_id=p.id AND a.status=activo) as active_member_count, (SELECT GROUP_CONCAT(CONCAT(adm.first_name, ' ', adm.last_name)) FROM project_managers pm JOIN administrators adm ON adm.id=pm.admin_id WHERE pm.project_id=p.id AND pm.is_primary=true) as lead_manager_name FROM projects p LEFT JOIN project_tags pt ON pt.project_id=p.id LEFT JOIN tags t ON t.id=pt.tag_id LEFT JOIN project_meeting_days pmd ON pmd.project_id=p.id WHERE p.slug=:slug AND p.is_visible=true GROUP BY p.id

endpoint.description: List visible events. Split into upcoming/past by caller or query param.
Full event detail. Powers /events/[slug] page.
All tags for filter dropdowns on projects/events pages.
CMS content for public page sections. Powers all site_content reads in SvelteKit load() functions.
endpoint.query-logic: home_hero → SELECT * FROM home_hero WHERE is_active=true LIMIT 1 dlab_identity → SELECT * FROM dlab_identity WHERE is_active=true LIMIT 1 value_propositions → SELECT * FROM value_propositions WHERE is_visible=true ORDER BY sort_order ASC participation_steps → SELECT * FROM participation_steps WHERE is_visible=true ORDER BY step_number ASC contact_info → SELECT * FROM contact_info WHERE is_active=true LIMIT 1 social_links → SELECT * FROM social_links WHERE is_visible=true ORDER BY sort_order ASC interest_form_config → SELECT * FROM interest_form_config WHERE is_active=true LIMIT 1

endpoint.description: List collaborators with filters. Powers /admin/colaboradores page.
Full collaborator profile for admin review. Includes applications, assignments, status history from audit_log.

Change collaborator trajectory_status. Triggers emails on specific transitions. Valid transitions (per entity model ENUM): nuevo → en_revision → contactado → vinculado → inactivo Any status → inactivo (rejection/deactivation)

field.name: reason
field.note: Included in rejection email body if new_status=inactivo

side-effects.effect: UPDATE collaborators SET trajectory_status=new_status, updated_at=NOW()
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=status_change
If new_status IN (contactado): email.sendCollaboratorApproved() — regenerate activation token if expired
If new_status = inactivo AND previous was nuevo/en_revision: email.sendCollaboratorRejected(reason)

endpoint.description: Add admin note to collaborator (stored in audit_log with action=update and description as note content).

side-effects.effect: INSERT INTO audit_log: actor_type=administrator, actor_id=req.admin.id, action=update, entity_type=collaborators, entity_id=id, description=content

endpoint.description: Review application. If approved: atomic stored procedure creates assignment and updates collaborator trajectory_status to vinculado.

responses.response: status is already aprobada or rechazada

side-effects.effect: UPDATE applications SET status, reviewed_at=NOW(), approver_admin_id=req.admin.id, admin_notes [, approved_at or rejected_at]

If aprobada: CALL sp_application_approve(application_id, admin_id) — atomic: 1. UPDATE applications.status = aprobada, approved_at = NOW() 2. INSERT INTO assignments (collaborator_id, project_id, application_id, assigned_by_admin_id, status=activo) 3. UPDATE projects SET current_collaborator_count = current_collaborator_count + 1 4. UPDATE collaborators SET trajectory_status = vinculado WHERE id = collaborator_id All in a single transaction — rollback on any failure.
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=approve or reject

endpoint.description: Manually assign collaborator to project (bypass application flow).

responses.response: Active assignment already exists for this collaborator+project pair

side-effects.effect: INSERT INTO assignments (assigned_by_admin_id=req.admin.id, application_id=NULL, status=activo)
UPDATE projects SET current_collaborator_count = current_collaborator_count + 1
UPDATE collaborators SET trajectory_status=vinculado IF not already vinculado
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=assign

endpoint.description: End an assignment. Auto-demotes collaborator to inactivo if no remaining active assignments exist for that collaborator.

side-effects.effect: UPDATE assignments SET status=new_status, ended_at, end_reason
UPDATE projects SET current_collaborator_count = MAX(0, current_collaborator_count - 1)
Check remaining active assignments: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM assignments WHERE collaborator_id=X AND status=activo
If count = 0: UPDATE collaborators SET trajectory_status=inactivo
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=unassign

endpoint.description: Update assignment metadata (role, pause/resume).

route-group.note: Image upload is separate — POST /api/media/upload (resource_type=project). Returns public_url to be saved in header_image_url field here.

endpoint.description: Create project. header_image_url is a Supabase public URL returned by /api/media/upload.

field.name: slug
field.note: Auto-generated from title if omitted. Must be unique.

field.name: header_image_url
field.note: Public URL from Supabase — returned by POST /api/media/upload

field.name: manager_admin_ids
field.note: Admins to insert into project_managers. First item = is_primary.

side-effects.effect: INSERT INTO projects (created_by_admin_id=req.admin.id)
INSERT INTO project_tags for each tag_id
INSERT INTO project_managers for each manager_admin_id (first = is_primary=true)
INSERT INTO project_meeting_days
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=create

responses.response: { "data": ProjectAdminDetail — includes hidden fields, application stats, manager list }

endpoint.description: Full update. Same body schema as POST. Tags and meeting_days are fully replaced.

side-effects.effect: UPDATE projects
DELETE + re-INSERT project_tags, project_meeting_days, project_managers
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=update

endpoint.guard: Cannot delete if active assignments exist → 422 ERR_PROJECT_HAS_ACTIVE_ASSIGNMENTS

side-effects.effect: INSERT INTO audit_log: action=delete
Cascade: project_tags, project_meeting_days, project_managers deleted via FK CASCADE or explicit DELETE

route-group.note: Image upload for events → POST /api/media/upload (resource_type=event) Bucket: SUPABASE_BUCKET_EVENTS = pmi-frontend-events-images Events support two image fields: header_image_url and banner_image_url.

field.name: header_image_url
field.note: Supabase public URL from POST /api/media/upload (resource_type=event, bucket=pmi-frontend-events-images)

field.name: banner_image_url
field.note: Secondary image — same bucket as header_image_url

side-effects.effect: INSERT INTO events (created_by_admin_id=req.admin.id)
INSERT INTO event_tags, event_managers
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=create

endpoint.description: Full update — same body as POST. Tags and managers fully replaced.

side-effects.effect: UPDATE events; DELETE + re-INSERT event_tags, event_managers
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=update
INSERT INTO audit_log: action=delete

endpoint.description: All tags with computed usage counts.
endpoint.query: SELECT t.*, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM collaborator_tags WHERE tag_id=t.id) as collaborator_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM project_tags WHERE tag_id=t.id) as project_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM event_tags WHERE tag_id=t.id) as event_count FROM tags t ORDER BY t.category, t.name

side-effects.effect: INSERT INTO tags (is_system=false, created_by_admin_id=req.admin.id)

endpoint.guard: is_system=true → 422 ERR_SYSTEM_TAG (cannot delete seeded system tags)

endpoint.description:
Upload image to Supabase Storage via IStorageProvider interface. resource_type determines bucket: project → SUPABASE_BUCKET_PROJECTS (pmi-frontend-proj-images) event → SUPABASE_BUCKET_EVENTS (pmi-frontend-events-images)
No files are ever written to the Express server filesystem. multer memoryStorage provides req.file.buffer which is passed directly to IStorageProvider.upload(bucket, path, buffer, mimetype).
The returned public_url should be saved by the caller in the project or event header_image_url / banner_image_url field via the respective admin endpoint.
media_assets table is updated to track file metadata.

field.name: image
field.note: Image file — validated by multer fileFilter against UPLOAD_ALLOWED_TYPES

field.name: resource_id
field.note: If provided and resource has existing image path in media_assets, old file is deleted from Supabase before uploading new one.

endpoint.processing: 1. multer validates file size (<= UPLOAD_MAX_SIZE_MB MB) and MIME type. 2. Resolve bucket: resource_type=project → SUPABASE_BUCKET_PROJECTS, event → SUPABASE_BUCKET_EVENTS. 3. Build object path: {resource_type}/{uuidv4()}-{sanitize(originalname)}.{ext} 4. If resource_id provided: lookup existing media_assets record, call IStorageProvider.delete(bucket, old_path). 5. Call IStorageProvider.upload(bucket, path, req.file.buffer, req.file.mimetype). 6. INSERT INTO media_assets (original_filename, stored_filename=path, public_url, storage_type=cloud, mime_type, file_size_bytes, entity_type=resource_type, entity_id=resource_id, alt_text, uploaded_by_admin_id=req.admin.id). 7. Return { public_url, path, media_asset_id }.

endpoint.description: Delete a stored image from Supabase and remove media_assets record.

field.name: path
field.note: Object path as stored in media_assets.stored_filename, e.g. projects/uuid-filename.webp

responses.response: media_assets record not found for given path

side-effects.effect: IStorageProvider.delete(bucket, path)
DELETE FROM media_assets WHERE stored_filename=path

endpoint.description: List all uploaded media assets, optionally filtered by entity.
Get current content for a CMS section. Admin version may include inactive records.
Replace full section content. Body schema is section-specific (validated per section). Sets updated_by_admin_id=req.admin.id, updated_at=NOW().

schema.fields: headline(str,required), subheadline(str), primary_cta_label(str,required), primary_cta_url(str,required), secondary_cta_label(str), secondary_cta_url(str), background_image_url(url), is_active(bool)
contact_email(email), contact_phone(str), physical_location(str), maps_url(url), cta_headline(str), cta_description(text), is_active(bool)
page_headline(str,required), page_description(text), success_message(text,required), success_email_subject(str), success_email_body(text), is_active(bool)

side-effects.effect: INSERT INTO audit_log: action=update, entity_type=section name

endpoint.description: Add a new value proposition card.
Update a value proposition card.

field.name: ordered_ids
field.note: Full ordered list of value_proposition IDs. Updates sort_order for each.

dep.name: mysql2
dep.note: Promise-based pool. Raw SQL + stored procedure calls via pool.execute()

dep.name: crypto
dep.note: Node.js built-in — used for HMAC token generation (no install needed)

dep.name: zod
dep.note: Schema validation for request bodies and env vars

dep.name: uuid
dep.note: uuidv4() for storage object path generation

dep.name: @azure/storage-blob
dep.note: Install when STORAGE_PROVIDER=azure. Remove stub from AzureStorageProvider.js

error.description: JWT verification failed or HMAC mismatch
Refresh token reuse detected — all sessions revoked
Email exists but not an administrator
Account already active — duplicate activation attempt
No file attached to upload request
project or event slug already exists
Collaborator already applied to this project
Active assignment already exists for collaborator+project
Collaborator trajectory_status does not allow applying
Requested trajectory_status transition is not allowed
Cannot delete project with active assignments
Cannot delete tag that is referenced by collaborators, projects, or events
Supabase returned an error on upload
Supabase returned an error on delete
Rate limit exceeded on auth route
Unhandled exception — details hidden in production

procedure.name: sp_application_approve
procedure.params: IN p_application_id INT, IN p_admin_id INT
procedure.rollback: On any error: ROLLBACK; re-throw to Express errorHandler

procedure.name: sp_register_collaborator
procedure.params: All collaborator fields as IN params
